operating_state: Net sales increased 5% to $80.2 billion driven by 7% organic growth across all segments, with 4% pricing increases and 2% volume growth partially offset by foreign exchange headwinds. Health Care delivered strongest performance with double-digit organic growth while Beauty lagged with low single-digit growth. Operations span approximately 180 countries with Walmart representing 15% of total sales, while the company suspended Russian operations beginning March 2022.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed 380 basis points to 47.4% as commodity cost inflation of 390 basis points overwhelmed 180 basis points of pricing benefits, with additional pressure from unfavorable product mix. SG&A expenses improved 240 basis points to 25.2% of sales through overhead cost savings and reduced advertising spend, resulting in operating margin declining 140 basis points to 22.2%. Purchase commitments under take-or-pay agreements total $2.8 billion.
material_events: Completed $10 billion share repurchase program and maintained 66th consecutive annual dividend increase to $3.52 per share with total dividend payments of $8.8 billion. David Taylor retired as Executive Chairman with Jon Moeller appointed Chairman while retaining President and CEO roles. Employee count increased 5% to approximately 106,000 driven by business growth while the company reduced its Russian product portfolio due to the Ukraine war.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation reduced net earnings by $274 million with operations outside the U.S. generating over 50% of annual sales, while currency exchange controls exist in Nigeria, Turkey, Argentina and Egypt. Russia-Ukraine war creates ongoing uncertainty affecting supply chains with Russia representing less than 2% of net sales. Commodity cost inflation particularly in oil-derived materials and pulp along with transportation cost increases of 60 basis points materially impacted margins.
forward_outlook: Management maintains long-term growth algorithm targeting organic sales growth above market rates, mid-to-high single-digit core EPS growth, and adjusted free cash flow productivity of 90% or greater. Capital expenditures increased to 3.9% of sales supporting capacity expansion and innovation initiatives while strategic focus continues on environmental sustainability, digital capabilities, and supply chain resilience.
uncertainty_profile: Shave Care reporting unit and Gillette intangible asset face elevated impairment risk with fair value cushion at approximately 5% above $14.1 billion carrying value, vulnerable to prolonged pandemic effects or war escalation. Uncertain tax positions liability totaled $583 million with audits underway across multiple jurisdictions. Legal proceedings span antitrust, product liability, environmental and patent matters though management assesses ultimate resolution will not materially affect financial position.
